articles.title: Arman’s Advice? Don’t Order Matcha #ufc
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan might be one of the most dangerous fighters in MMA—but this version of him? This is something else entirely. In this clip, Tsarukyan steps out of fight mode and into full personality mode, giving dating advice that’s equal parts hilarious and completely unfiltered. From telling a guy to ditch the matcha and order black coffee, to insisting you go full caveman at dinner—this is Arman like you’ve never seen him before. The moment isn’t just funny—it shows a different side of a top UFC contender. The confidence, the blunt delivery, the “take it or leave it” attitude—it’s all part of what makes fighters stand out beyond the octagon. And in today’s fight game, personality matters just as much as performance. Whether you agree with the advice or not… you’re definitely not forgetting it. This is the kind of content that builds stars—raw, real, and impossible to ignore. Subscribe for more MMA clips, fighter moments, and behind-the-scenes personality you won’t see anywhere else. #ArmanTsarukyan #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #MMANews #CombatSports #FunnyMMA

articles.title: 'LOCATION SENT!' Strickland BLASTS Khamzat Chimaev for Ducking Street Fight 😤
articles.description: The buildup to UFC 328 is getting heated as Khamzat Chimaev prepares to defend his belt against Sean Strickland — and the mind games are already in full swing. Strickland is calling him out for not showing up after “dropping locations,” while Chimaev’s reputation as one of the most dangerous fighters in the world continues to loom large. Is this just classic trash talk… or is there real tension here? We break down: The street fight talk 👀 Chimaev’s wrestling vs Strickland’s defense Conditioning, weight cut & fight dynamics Why this fight is NOT as one-sided as people think This one is getting personal… and it’s shaping up to be a massive clash at UFC 328. articles.title: Colby Covington says CREATING “Chaos” Colby SAVED HIS CAREER | The Daniel Cormier Show
articles.description: Daniel Cormier is joined by Colby Covington, where he talks about making a CHANGE in his career to make himself more popular. Colby remembers creating Colby “Chaos” Covington by talking trash to any and all opponents in the UFC. Colby remembers beating the No. 6 fighter in the weight class, and getting NO love, and finding out that the UFC was going to cut him because they didn’t find him interesting. Colby knew he had to save his career, and decided to play the villain role, and he became one of the most polarizing fighters in the UFC. articles.title: Merab’s Training Finally Backfired
articles.description: Merab Dvalishvili used to spar five hard rounds… on the day of his fight. And somehow, it worked — until it didn’t. Chael breaks down why that approach was never a good idea, even if it seemed to produce results. Because just because something hasn’t gone wrong yet… doesn’t mean it won’t. From fight-day preparation to the difference between overtraining and unnecessary training, this is a deeper look at what fighters should and shouldn’t be doing before they walk to the cage. There’s a fine line between working harder than everyone else… and doing something that hurts you when it matters most. And when fighters start talking about “training smarter, not harder”… that might sound good — but it can also be the beginning of a decline. So what changed with Merab? And did he actually fix the problem — or is there a bigger mistake waiting around the corner? Because in fighting, it’s not just about how hard you train… it’s about when. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #MerabDvalishvili #ChaelSonnen #FightTraining 0:00 Merab’s Fight Day Habit 1:14 Why It’s So Unusual 3:30 Overtraining vs Too Much 5:06 Timing Is Everything 7:33 The Real Risk Ahead Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Daniel Cormier WARNS Arman Tsarukyan to not blow title shot + What Holloway told DC about McGregor
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan got KICKED OFF his flight, and Max Holloway told DC he wants Conor McGregor, so Daniel Cormier IS BACK with his instant reaction to the biggest storylines in the UFC and MMA. DC warns Arman Tsarukyan to NOT MESS UP his title shot in the UFC, as many project he'll face the winner of the Justin Gaethje vs. Ilia Topuria championship fight at the White House. Arman was recently kicked off a flight for using his phone during takeoff, as this is yet another mishap in Tsarukyan's step towards a title. Cormier also shares that he had a conversation with Max Holloway recently, and that he's fully set on fighting Conor McGregor next after losing the BMF title fight to Charles Oliveira. Don't miss DC give his take on Max fighting Conor next, Arman's antics outside the octagon, and so much more! All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms! articles.title: Pereira vs Hokit Would Be Chaos #ufc
articles.description: Alex Pereira vs Chase Hooper? It sounds wild—but in this game, wild things happen all the time. In this clip, a chaotic but very real scenario is laid out. If plans fall apart—if a top contender can’t make the walk or a fight collapses late—opportunities open up fast. And that’s where someone like Hooper could suddenly find himself stepping into the biggest moment of his career, possibly even on a massive stage. This isn’t about predicting the fight—it’s about understanding how unpredictable MMA matchmaking can be. Fighters go from under the radar to center stage overnight. And when you’ve got a champion like Pereira waiting, the door is always open for someone to take a shot. That’s what makes this sport different. It only takes one moment, one call, one opportunity—and everything changes. Laugh at the matchup if you want… but don’t be surprised if something just as crazy actually happens. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #AlexPereira #ChaseHooper #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: Rousey Took The Spot, Let’s Be Honest #ufc
articles.description: Ronda Rousey built a powerful narrative—but does it actually hold up? In this clip, the spotlight turns to the contradiction behind the “us vs. the UFC” angle. On one hand, it’s a compelling story—standing with the fighters, backing the so-called “little guy,” and pushing against the system. But on the other hand, there’s a reality that can’t be ignored. Rousey was the biggest star on the card. The highest-paid. The main attraction. And when the top spot was available, she took it. There’s nothing wrong with that—but it doesn’t exactly align with the idea of stepping aside or lifting others up. That’s where the tension comes in. You can dominate the spotlight, or you can position yourself as the voice of the overlooked—but trying to do both creates a narrative that doesn’t quite match the facts. This isn’t about criticism—it’s about clarity. In the fight game, perception matters just as much as performance. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the business behind the fights. #RondaRousey #UFC #MMA #FightBusiness #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: ‘Alex Pereira is FAT!?’ Fans PANIC at HEAVYWEIGHT Look | Khamzat Wants to KILL Strickland
articles.description: Fans are losing their minds over Alex Pereira looking “out of shape” ahead of the biggest fight of his career — but there’s a reality people are ignoring. Moving from 185 to 205 was one thing… jumping to heavyweight is a completely different challenge. You don’t stay shredded when you’re adding size for power, durability, and survival against true heavyweights. In this breakdown, we explain what’s actually happening with Pereira’s physique, the trade-offs of moving up in weight, and what it really means for his chances on fight night. Is he slower? Maybe. More powerful? Possibly. But “out of shape”? Not even close. 🔒 Become a Member for special Perks, Exclusive Content and ultra-exclusive Live Chats: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCDrG2_1TcVkXKXXsD6Kjwig/join ▶️ Follow Michael on Social: https://www.instagram.com/mikebisping https://www.twitter.com/bisping https://www.facebook.com/mikethecountbisping ▶️ Edited by John Brannigan: https://www.instagram.com/johnbranniganuk https://www.twitter.com/johnbranniganuk #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #ufcnews #mmanews #alexpereira #cirylgane #UFCFreedom250 #khamzat #khamzatchiamev #seanstrickland

articles.title: Ronda Compared Herself to Jon Jones?!
articles.description: Ronda Rousey got asked a simple question… and gave a very strategic answer. When a reporter brought up her age, Rousey didn’t say yes or no — instead, she flipped the question and pointed at Jon Jones. It shut the conversation down instantly… but it also revealed something much bigger. Chael breaks down why that move was so effective — and what Ronda is really trying to avoid. Because this isn’t just about age. It’s about positioning. It’s about narrative. And it’s about controlling which questions get asked — and which ones don’t. Chael explains why comparing herself to Jon Jones is a stretch on paper, but brilliant in practice. By associating herself with one of the greatest fighters ever, Ronda shifts the conversation away from uncomfortable truths and into a completely different lane. And that matters — because there’s a much harder question lurking beneath the surface: why is Ronda getting this spot at this stage of her career? From storytelling to self-promotion, this is a masterclass in controlling the narrative — whether you agree with it or not. Because in this sport, it’s not just about what you say…it’s about what you make people forget. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #JonJones #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Ronda Asked About Her Age 0:17 Brings Up Jon Jones 0:55 Why the Comparison Falls Apart 1:20 The Real Question Being Asked 2:14 The Answer She Can’t Give 3:04 Associating With Greatness 4:27 The Narrative She’s Building 5:05 The Truth She’s Avoiding Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Narrative Ulberg Must Own #ufc
articles.description: Carlos Ulberg did something that should define the entire narrative—he beat Jiří Procházka faster than Alex Pereira did. That’s not a small detail… that’s the story. But here’s the problem—stories in MMA don’t tell themselves. Fighters have to claim them, push them, and make the world pay attention. And if Ulberg doesn’t take control of that narrative right now, it risks fading away completely. In this clip, the bigger issue is laid out. Momentum doesn’t last forever. If this moment isn’t capitalized on, Ulberg could find himself sitting on the sidelines while the division moves on without him. And that’s the danger—even after a massive win. Because the same question still exists: what’s next? Whether it was Procházka or Ulberg holding the spotlight, the division still lacks clarity at the top. This is where champions separate themselves—not just by winning, but by owning the moment that comes after. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, sharp takes, and real fight game insight. #CarlosUlberg #UFC #MMA #JiriProchazka #AlexPereira #UFCLightHeavyweight #FightTalk

articles.title: 1,000,000 PPVs… On Netflix?!
articles.description: Eddie Hearn says he wants to fight Dana White… and even laid out the deal. According to Eddie, the fight would do a million pay-per-views easy — and both sides would walk away with massive money. On paper, it sounds big. Maybe even huge.But then the argument falls apart. Chael breaks down where Eddie was right, where he completely lost the thread, and why this entire situation says more than people realize. Because this isn’t really about a fight — it’s about frustration. When words stop working, things escalate. That’s how fights actually start. From calling out Dana directly to doubling down in interviews, Eddie didn’t just float an idea — he pushed it. And in doing so, he may have revealed what’s really going on behind the scenes. Is this about business? Competition? Or something bigger happening between boxing and the UFC? Because when a promoter starts talking like a fighter…something’s already gone wrong. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #Boxing #DanaWhite #EddieHearn #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Eddie Challenges Dana 0:32 The Million PPV Claim 1:07 Where It Falls Apart 2:11 The Reporter Problem 3:59 When Words Turn Into Fights Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Ask Uncle Chael: Islam, Conor & More
articles.description: It’s another round of Ask Uncle Chael — and nothing is off limits. From fight controversies to big-picture strategy, Chael takes on questions from fans and delivers his signature no-nonsense breakdowns. This episode covers everything from injury speculation in the octagon to the ongoing tension between Islam Makhachev and Ilia Topuria. Chael weighs in on whether fighters really compete injured, and why conversations around injuries — especially things like ACL tears — can be misleading without real evidence. He also dives into the narrative around Islam vs Topuria, separating what actually happened from how the story is being told. But the real gem comes when Chael explains the single most important principle of fight promotion — and why most people completely misunderstand it. According to Chael, big fights aren’t built on records… they’re built on stories. Plus, Chael gives his thoughts on Conor McGregor’s return timeline, potential future political candidates, and even throws his name into the mix for Real American Freestyle. If you want real answers — and a better understanding of how this sport actually works — this is the episode. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #IslamMakhachev #IliaTopuria #ConorMcGregor 0:00 Ask Uncle Chael Begins 0:06 Did Oberg Have an ACL Injury? 0:42 Why Injury Talk Gets Misleading 1:13 Fighting With Injuries Reality 1:47 Question on “Paying to Leave” States 2:25 Chael on Taxes & Government 3:47 Grappling A-Game Explained 4:20 Why Distance Kills Takedowns 5:00 The Only Takedown That Works 5:32 Why “Fabric” Matters in MMA 5:55 Dungeons & Dragons Joke 6:05 Islam vs Topuria Question 6:40 Did Islam Avoid the Fight? 7:29 Why Islam Said No 8:13 The Truth About That Decision 8:24 What Makes Fight Promotion Work 9:01 The 5 Ws Explained 9:28 Why Big Fights Are Just Stories 10:00 Conor McGregor Return Timeline 10:50 Don Jr. Political Question 12:00 Chael Calls Out RAF Opponent Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Josh Hokit has ARRIVED #ufc
articles.description: Chase Hooper just had his arrival moment—and it didn’t come the way you’d expect. In this clip, what looks like chaos on the surface turns into something much bigger. Hooper takes real damage, goes through a brutal fight, and still finds a way to make it work. Against a powerhouse like Curtis Blaydes—a physically imposing heavyweight—that kind of durability and grit tells you everything you need to know. But here’s where it gets interesting. It’s not just about winning or losing—it’s about moments. The optics, the story, the timing. From the intensity of the fight to the unexpected spotlight, everything came together in a way that elevated Hooper instantly. Sometimes it’s not clean. Sometimes it’s not pretty. But when a fighter can turn a messy, difficult situation into momentum—that’s when you know they’ve arrived. This is the fight game at its rawest: toughness, opportunity, and making something out of nothing. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #ChaseHooper #UFC #MMA #CurtisBlaydes #FightTalk #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: Blaydes Says He Won — Nobody Agrees
articles.description: Curtis Blaydes says he won the fight… but nobody in the building seemed to agree. After a Fight of the Night performance against Josh, Blaydes came out and made a surprising claim — that he believed he did enough to get his hand raised. Chael breaks down why that statement matters, and more importantly, what it’s supposed to accomplish. Because historically, when fighters push a “I won that fight” narrative, it comes with something else — controversy, crowd backlash, or a clear path to a rematch. But in this case? The 14,000 fans in attendance didn’t boo. They didn’t protest. They didn’t disagree with the judges at all. So now the question becomes: what is Blaydes really after? Is this about getting a rematch? Is it about positioning himself for a bigger opportunity? Or is it simply a fighter trying to reclaim a moment he feels slipped away? Chael dives into the scoring, the reality of how the fight played out, and why this kind of claim only works when the audience is on your side. Because in this sport, saying you won isn’t enough…you need people to believe you. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. 0:00 Curtis Blaydes Says He Won 0:16 Why That Statement Matters 0:51 Blaydes’ History Calling for Rematches 1:14 Why This Claim Is Stronger 1:34 Fight of the Night Context 1:56 The Missing Piece: Crowd Reaction 2:09 14,000 Fans Didn’t Disagree 2:41 Breaking Down the Fight Itself 2:56 The Wrestling Argument 3:09 Why That Isn’t Enough3:27 What Was Blaydes Trying to Do? 3:48 Rematch or Bigger Opportunity? 4:02 The Real Question Behind the Claim #UFC #MMA #CurtisBlaydes #ChaelSonnen #FightAnalysis Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Masvidal’s Story Doesn’t Add Up
articles.description: Jorge Masvidal was building something… and then he broke it. For months, Masvidal had people believing he was coming back — teasing fights, dropping hints, and keeping the story alive. Whether it was real or not didn’t matter. It was working. Until it didn’t. Chael Sonnen breaks down where Masvidal went wrong — and why the biggest mistake you can make in the fight business isn’t telling a story… it’s contradicting your own story. Because once that happens, the audience stops believing. From calling out Leon Edwards to abandoning the narrative he built, Masvidal may have accidentally shut down his own momentum. And that’s the lesson. In this game, you’re not just a fighter — you’re a performer. But the second you lose control of the story, the performance falls apart. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fighters that matter. #UFC #MMA #JorgeMasvidal #LeonEdwards #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Masvidal’s Story Was Working1:30 The Big Mistake3:00 The Leon Edwards Callout5:00 Why It Broke Everything7:00 The Real Lesson Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Real Reason This Fight Didn’t Sell #ufc
articles.description: This wasn’t just a slow night—it was a warning sign. When a fight card underperforms at the gate, there’s always a reason… and it’s not just about the fighters. In this clip, the real issue gets exposed. Despite the presence of high-level talent like Jiří Procházka and Carlos Ulberg, the event struggled to connect with fans. And the narrative coming out only adds fuel to the fire—did Ulberg win, or did Procházka simply lose? But the bigger takeaway goes beyond the result. Characters matter. Storylines matter. Procházka’s samurai persona is close to something special—but it’s missing two key elements: why the fight matters to him, and why he believes he’ll beat his opponent. Without that emotional connection, even elite fights can fall flat. This is the fight game behind the fight game. Skill gets you in the cage—but story sells the tickets. If the UFC wants bigger gates and stronger engagement, this is the blueprint they can’t ignore. Subscribe for more MMA insights, breakdowns, and real talk on what drives the fight business. #UFC #MMA #JiriProchazka #CarlosUlberg #FightBusiness #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: Colby Covington on White House card, Kamaru Usman & Jorge Masvidal, UFC VILLAN | Daniel Cormier
articles.description: Colby Covington officially makes his DEBUT on the Daniel Cormier SHOW! Colby goes in on being left off the White House card, and details his rivalries between him and UFC superstars Jorge Masvidal and Kamaru Usman. Covington also discusses his time in collegiate wrestling and how he became one of the best wrestlers in the country. Colby then reveals to DC that he was roommates with Jon Jones in college and talks about how it was to live with him. And don't miss Covington's thoughts on the White House card, President Donald Trump, his RAF wrestling career, the Justin Gaethje and Ilia Topuria fight, and so much more! All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms! articles.title: This Is Next-Level Fight Psychology #ufc
articles.description: This wasn’t just a performance—it was strategy on another level. One fighter, three completely different personalities… and every single one serves a purpose. In this clip, the breakdown dives into a unique psychological approach inside MMA. There’s the real version—calm, reserved, even a little nervous. Then there’s the fearless competitor, the version that shows up ready to fight anyone, anytime. And finally, there’s the street-level persona—raw, aggressive, and built to intimidate. Each version plays a role. Each one sends a different message. And together, they create unpredictability that’s nearly impossible to prepare for. Opponents don’t just have to game plan for skill—they have to figure out which version of the fighter they’re actually facing. That’s where the brilliance comes in. This isn’t just about fighting—it’s about controlling perception, building identity, and using psychology as a weapon. In today’s fight game, standing out matters. And this might be one of the smartest ways to do it. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, fight psychology, and real insight into the game behind the game. #UFC #MMA #FightPsychology #CombatSports #FightTalk #MindGames #MMAAnalysis

articles.title: Chael’s Wild Pereira vs Hokit Prediction
articles.description: Alex Pereira vs Josh Hokit sounds crazy…But Chael Sonnen says it might not be. In this breakdown, Chael walks through a scenario where everything lines up — and suddenly, one of the most unexpected fights in the sport becomes very real. From card placement to timing to the chaos that always surrounds big events, the path is there… if a few things break the right way. And with Pereira tied up in multiple potential matchups — and the heavyweight division constantly shifting — opportunities like this can appear fast. Because in this sport, it’s not always about rankings. It’s about being in the right place at the right time. And right now, Josh Hokit might be exactly that.
